message:
Add Splunk HEC metadata and raw endpoint support (#6074) ## 🔍 Problem - The neo `to_splunk` path could only send HEC event envelopes. - Users could not set HEC `_time`, attach indexed HEC fields, or send already-formatted log text to Splunk's raw HEC endpoint. - Raw HEC metadata is request-level, so batching has to respect metadata changes. - The legacy `to_splunk` implementation should remain untouched. ## 🛠️ Solution - Add neo `to_splunk` support for `time=...` and event-mode `fields={...}`. - Keep HEC event envelopes as the default; `event=...` selects the envelope payload and defaults to `this`. - Add raw HEC mode with the mutually exclusive `raw=` option instead of an endpoint selector. - Send raw events to `/services/collector/raw` with request-level metadata query parameters and a generated request channel. - Split adjacent raw batches when request metadata changes, preserve raw pending events across compression failures, and keep raw metadata aligned when null raw rows are skipped. - Add validation tests, live Splunk coverage, and a changelog entry. ## 💬 Review - Check the final `event=` / `raw=` UX and validation behavior. - Check raw batching around metadata changes and null raw rows. - Check `time` and `fields` validation, warning, and omission behavior. - The `contrib/tenzir-plugins` submodule points at the companion plugin commit on `tenzir/tenzir-plugins:topic/to-splunk-improvements`. 🧩 Plugin PR: tenzir/tenzir-plugins#520
📚 Docs PR: tenzir/docs#278
✅ Closes TNZ-517, TNZ-518, TNZ-519

files.patch:
@@ -1 +1 @@
-Subproject commit db42f7eab671f09bf24fce51db618b759c7dc510
+Subproject commit f652a67d68dc3270da675132c9d0346579235837

@@ -18,6 +18,8 @@
 - source: optional source filter.
 - sourcetype: optional sourcetype filter.
 - host: optional host filter.
+- earliest_time: optional search lower bound, defaults to -10m.
+- latest_time: optional search upper bound, defaults to now.
 - search: optional full Splunk search query, overriding generated filters.
 """
 
@@ -72,6 +74,8 @@ class SplunkAssertions:
     source: str | None = None
     sourcetype: str | None = None
     host: str | None = None
+    earliest_time: str = "-10m"
+    latest_time: str = "now"
     search: str | None = None
 
     def __post_init__(self) -> None:
@@ -211,14 +215,20 @@ def _make_search(assertions: SplunkAssertions) -> str:
     return "search " + " ".join(terms)
 
 
-def _run_search(mgmt_port: int, search: str) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
+def _run_search(
+    mgmt_port: int,
+    search: str,
+    *,
+    earliest_time: str,
+    latest_time: str,
+) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
     url = f"https://127.0.0.1:{mgmt_port}/services/search/jobs/export"
     payload = urllib.parse.urlencode(
         {
             "search": search,
             "output_mode": "json",
-            "earliest_time": "-10m",
-            "latest_time": "now",
+            "earliest_time": earliest_time,
+            "latest_time": latest_time,
         }
     ).encode()
     request = urllib.request.Request(
@@ -265,7 +275,12 @@ def _verify_search(
     while True:
         search_failed = False
         try:
-            results = _run_search(mgmt_port, search)
+            results = _run_search(
+                mgmt_port,
+                search,
+                earliest_time=assertions.earliest_time,
+                latest_time=assertions.latest_time,
+            )
             last_results = results
             last_error = None
             result_texts = [_result_text(result) for result in results]

@@ -0,0 +1,19 @@
+---
+assertions:
+  fixtures:
+    splunk:
+      count: 1
+      search: 'search index="main" source="tenzir-test-fields" tenant::acme wins::regionals'
+      contains:
+        - fields-event
+---
+
+from {message: "fields-event", tenant_id: "acme", win: "regionals"}
+to_splunk env("SPLUNK_HEC_URL"),
+          hec_token=env("SPLUNK_HEC_TOKEN"),
+          event={message: message},
+          fields={tenant: tenant_id, wins: [win]},
+          source="tenzir-test-fields",
+          sourcetype="_json",
+          index=env("SPLUNK_INDEX"),
+          compress=false

@@ -0,0 +1,23 @@
+---
+assertions:
+  fixtures:
+    splunk:
+      count: 2
+      source: tenzir-test-raw
+      sourcetype: tenzir:test:raw
+      contains:
+        - raw-alpha
+        - raw-beta
+---
+
+from {message: "raw-alpha"},
+     {message: "raw-beta"}
+timestamp = now().format_time("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
+message = timestamp + " " + message
+to_splunk env("SPLUNK_HEC_URL"),
+          hec_token=env("SPLUNK_HEC_TOKEN"),
+          raw=message,
+          source="tenzir-test-raw",
+          sourcetype="tenzir:test:raw",
+          index=env("SPLUNK_INDEX"),
+          compress=false

@@ -0,0 +1,24 @@
+---
+assertions:
+  fixtures:
+    splunk:
+      count: 2
+      source: tenzir-test-raw-split
+      sourcetype: tenzir:test:raw-split
+      contains:
+        - raw-split-alpha
+        - raw-split-beta
+        - tenzir-raw-a
+        - tenzir-raw-b
+---
+
+from {message: "raw-split-alpha", h: "tenzir-raw-a"},
+     {message: "raw-split-beta", h: "tenzir-raw-b"}
+to_splunk env("SPLUNK_HEC_URL"),
+          hec_token=env("SPLUNK_HEC_TOKEN"),
+          raw=message,
+          host=h,
+          source="tenzir-test-raw-split",
+          sourcetype="tenzir:test:raw-split",
+          index=env("SPLUNK_INDEX"),
+          compress=false

@@ -0,0 +1,22 @@
+---
+assertions:
+  fixtures:
+    splunk:
+      count: 1
+      host: tenzir-raw-aligned
+      source: tenzir-test-raw-null-alignment
+      sourcetype: tenzir:test:raw-null-alignment
+      contains:
+        - raw-null-aligned
+---
+
+from {message: null.string(), h: "tenzir-raw-skipped"},
+     {message: "raw-null-aligned", h: "tenzir-raw-aligned"}
+to_splunk env("SPLUNK_HEC_URL"),
+          hec_token=env("SPLUNK_HEC_TOKEN"),
+          raw=message,
+          host=h,
+          source="tenzir-test-raw-null-alignment",
+          sourcetype="tenzir:test:raw-null-alignment",
+          index=env("SPLUNK_INDEX"),
+          compress=false

@@ -0,0 +1,22 @@
+---
+assertions:
+  fixtures:
+    splunk:
+      count: 1
+      source: tenzir-test-time
+      sourcetype: tenzir:test:time
+      earliest_time: "1735689599"
+      latest_time: "1735689601"
+      contains:
+        - time-event
+        - "2025-01-01"
+---
+
+from {message: "time-event", ts: 2025-01-01T00:00:00Z}
+to_splunk env("SPLUNK_HEC_URL"),
+          hec_token=env("SPLUNK_HEC_TOKEN"),
+          time=ts,
+          source="tenzir-test-time",
+          sourcetype="tenzir:test:time",
+          index=env("SPLUNK_INDEX"),
+          compress=false

@@ -0,0 +1,8 @@
+---
+error: true
+---
+
+to_splunk "https://127.0.0.1:8088",
+          hec_token="token",
+          event=this,
+          raw=message

@@ -0,0 +1,9 @@
+error: `event` and `raw` are mutually exclusive
+ --> tests/operators/to_splunk_validation/error_event_raw_mutually_exclusive.tql:8:15
+  |
+8 |           raw=message
+  |               ^^^^^^^ 
+  ⋮
+7 |           event=this,
+  |                 ---- 
+  |

@@ -0,0 +1,8 @@
+---
+error: true
+---
+
+to_splunk "https://127.0.0.1:8088",
+          hec_token="token",
+          raw="message",
+          fields={tenant: "acme"}

@@ -0,0 +1,9 @@
+error: `fields` is not supported with `raw`
+ --> tests/operators/to_splunk_validation/error_fields_raw.tql:8:18
+  |
+8 |           fields={tenant: "acme"}
+  |                  ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ 
+  ⋮
+7 |           raw="message",
+  |               --------- 
+  |

@@ -0,0 +1,5 @@
+from {message: "this event does not fit", tenant: "acme"}
+to_splunk "http://127.0.0.1:8088",
+          hec_token="token",
+          fields={tenant: tenant, nested: {value: "unsupported"}},
+          max_content_length=1

@@ -0,0 +1,14 @@
+warning: `fields.nested` must be a string or list of strings; omitting field
+ --> tests/operators/to_splunk_validation/fields_wrong_type.tql:4:18
+  |
+4 |           fields={tenant: tenant, nested: {value: "unsupported"}},
+  |                  ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ 
+  |
+
+warning: event too large for given `max_content_length`
+ --> tests/operators/to_splunk_validation/fields_wrong_type.tql:5:30
+  |
+5 |           max_content_length=1
+  |                              ~ 
+  |
+  = note: serialized event size was `111`

@@ -0,0 +1,4 @@
+from {message: {nested: "not raw text"}}
+to_splunk "http://127.0.0.1:8088",
+          hec_token="token",
+          raw=message

@@ -0,0 +1,6 @@
+warning: `raw` must be `string`, got `record`
+ --> tests/operators/to_splunk_validation/raw_event_must_be_string.tql:4:15
+  |
+4 |           raw=message
+  |               ~~~~~~~ 
+  |

@@ -0,0 +1,5 @@
+from {message: "this raw event does not fit"}
+to_splunk "http://127.0.0.1:8088",
+          hec_token="token",
+          raw=message,
+          max_content_length=1

@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+warning: event too large for given `max_content_length`
+ --> tests/operators/to_splunk_validation/raw_event_too_large.tql:5:30
+  |
+5 |           max_content_length=1
+  |                              ~ 
+  |
+  = note: serialized event size was `27`

@@ -0,0 +1,5 @@
+from {message: "this event does not fit", ts: "not an epoch"}
+to_splunk "http://127.0.0.1:8088",
+          hec_token="token",
+          time=ts,
+          max_content_length=1

@@ -0,0 +1,14 @@
+warning: `time` did not evaluate to a `time` or numeric epoch timestamp
+ --> tests/operators/to_splunk_validation/time_wrong_type.tql:4:16
+  |
+4 |           time=ts,
+  |                ~~ 
+  |
+
+warning: event too large for given `max_content_length`
+ --> tests/operators/to_splunk_validation/time_wrong_type.tql:5:30
+  |
+5 |           max_content_length=1
+  |                              ~ 
+  |
+  = note: serialized event size was `88`
